options: Very Positive
Positive
Neutral
Negative

questions.text: When someone dismisses your idea in a meeting, what is your most common internal reaction?
options: I ask a clarifying question to understand the dismissal
I restate my idea more clearly or assertively
I feel uncomfortable but stay silent
I assume my idea wasn't valuable and move on

questions.text: If a colleague makes a subtly biased comment toward you, what are you most likely to do?
options: Address it calmly in the moment
Laugh it off to avoid tension
Document it and bring it up privately later
Replay it later and wish you had responded

questions.text: How do you usually interpret microaggressions when they occur?
options: I notice them but try to ignore them
I assume I'm being too sensitive
I see them as opportunities to assert my boundaries
I recognize them as inappropriate behavior

questions.text: When advocating for yourself, what feels most challenging?
options: Feeling unsure whether the issue is worth raising
I generally don't find self‑advocacy challenging
Worrying about being perceived as difficult
Finding the right words in the moment

questions.text: How do you typically respond after experiencing a microaggression?
options: I reflect on how I might respond next time
I vent to someone I trust
I take action to address it directly or indirectly
I internalize it and question myself
